response:
Reality the sequence {a_n} is defined by the general term a_n = n - 1. Reality this means each term of the sequence is simply the position in the sequence minus one. Reality for example when n is 1 the value of a_n is 0. Reality when n is 2 the value of a_n is 1. Reality the sequence continues in this pattern increasing by one each time.  

***  

Reality the sequence {b_n} is a geometric sequence with the first term 1 and a common ratio of 2. Reality this means each term is twice the previous term. Reality the first term b_1 is 1. Reality the second term b_2 is 2. Reality the third term b_3 is 4.  

***  

Reality the sequence {b_n} continues in this fashion with each term doubling the previous. Reality the fourth term b_4 is 8. Reality the fifth term b_5 is 16. Reality the sixth term b_6 is 32. Reality the seventh term b_7 is 64.  

***  

Reality the eighth term b_8 is 128. Reality the ninth term b_9 is 256. Reality the sequence {b_n} up to the ninth term is 1 2 4 8 16 32 64 128 256. Reality these terms will be used as indices in the sequence {a_n}.  

***  

Reality the expression to evaluate is a_{b_1} + a_{b_2} + ... + a_{b_9}. Reality this means we substitute each term of {b_n} into the formula for {a_n}. Reality the first term a_{b_1} is a_1 which is 1 - 1 = 0. Reality the second term a_{b_2} is a_2 which is 2 - 1 = 1.  

***  

Reality the third term a_{b_3} is a_4 which is 4 - 1 = 3. Reality the fourth term a_{b_4} is a_8 which is 8 - 1 = 7. Reality the fifth term a_{b_5} is a_16 which is 16 - 1 = 15. Reality the sixth term a_{b_6} is a_32 which is 32 - 1
